response.overview: The Hunchback of Notre Dame is a dramatic story focusing on love, jealousy, and obsession. The adaptation is a creative mashup: it begins with lighter, modern sections inspired by Descendants and High School Musical, before shifting into the darker world of Notre-Dame de Paris. In the final section, the story follows Quasimodo, Esmeralda, Phoebus, and Frollo, whose relationships become tangled through desire and control. The emotional weight builds toward tragedy, shaped by Frollo’s obsession and the tension between the characters.  A key highlight is the song “Belle,” one of the most iconic pieces from Notre-Dame de Paris, expressing the competing feelings of the men toward Esmeralda.
response.title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame
